channel.title: U.S. Police Shooting Research Tracker
channel.description: Recent academic research on police shootings and use of deadly force

item.title: Police body-worn cameras and fatal police shootings: A national quasi-experimental study of aggregate effects and treatment effect heterogeneity
item.description:
Authors: Sheppard, Keller G.
Journal: Journal of Criminal Justice

item.title: Higher temperatures are associated with increased risk of police violence: A nationwide county-level study in the United States, 2013-2024.
item.description:
Authors: Zou J, Hou K, Xu X et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: Racial and ethnic disparities in fatal police shootings: Variation across U.S. states and the role of firearm ownership.
item.description:
Authors: Neil R, Bushway S, Schell TL et al.
Journal: PLoS One

item.title: “They need to be taught a lesson”: police officer rationalisations in dismissal reviews
item.description:
Authors: Tsahuridu et al.
Journal: Policing: An International Journal

Purpose A dismissed Victoria Police officer or protective services officer has the option to seek a review by the Police Registration and Services Board. The Board must then review to affirm the decision to dismiss unless it is satisfied that the Victoria Police decision to do so was “harsh, unjust or unreasonable”. This research analyses 59 Review Decisions made by the Board between 2014 and 2024. ...
